description: Add a dedicated Relay page with a hero block, short bio, mission, how-I-can-help bullets, and a playful Q&A to introduce Relay as the blog’s resident assistant.

userStories.title: Add Relay page route
userStories.description: As a reader, I want a dedicated Relay page so I can learn who Relay is.
acceptanceCriteria: A new page/route exists for Relay (e.g., /relay or a route consistent with the site’s routing)
The page is reachable from the site’s navigation or another clear entry point
The page renders without errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create Relay hero block
userStories.description: As a reader, I want a hero section that introduces Relay at a glance so I immediately understand the page’s purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero block includes Relay name, a short subtitle/tagline, and a brief intro sentence
Hero block is visually prominent at the top of the page
Hero block includes a CTA or jump link to the “How I can help” section
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add bio, mission, and help bullets
userStories.description: As a reader, I want a short bio and a clear list of ways Relay can help so I know what to expect.
acceptanceCriteria: Bio is 2–4 sentences and introduces Relay’s role
Mission statement is a single concise sentence
“How I can help” section contains 3–5 bullet items
Tone is playful and witty while remaining clear
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Relay Q&A section
userStories.description: As a reader, I want a short Q&A so I can quickly understand Relay’s personality and boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Q&A includes 3–5 questions with short answers
At least one Q&A addresses what Relay does NOT do
Tone matches the playful, witty style of the page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
